_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing data classification schemes that balance organizational needs for data access with protection requirements, and that can be practically implemented and maintained across diverse data environments.
comments: Information ordering maps to WIDAI classification, taxonomy design, and data organization skills. DG-S-001 (classification schemes), DA-K-001 (data modeling), DQ-K-001 (quality dimensions). Organizing information according to rules is core to data governance.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score reflects a genuine but bounded intersection. The overlapping concepts are detectable but represent a subset of what each description covers independently.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8362 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.76%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is the expected NLI outcome for Intersects relationships — the descriptions share conceptual territory without one implying the other.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.2035) is substantially lower than the STS cross-encoder (0.4152), a gap of 0.21. This divergence is diagnostic: the cross-encoder captures cross-text semantic interactions that the bi-encoder's independent encoding misses. The relationship between these descriptions depends on contextual meaning that only emerges when both texts are processed together.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Minimal lexical overlap (2.44% of tokens shared). The strong contrast between near-zero lexical overlap and the STS semantic score confirms this is a cross-domain relationship — the concepts are related through meaning, not vocabulary. This is expected for O*NET-to-WIDAI comparisons where general occupational language maps to domain-specific data/AI terminology.
